description: Tests organ-specific reference direction (ref_direc) selection. Each organ has a clinically-preferred approach direction to avoid OARs. Verifies the new _resolve_ref_direc logic (T1.4, planning_pipeline.py).

cases.input: Plan trajectory for a pancreatic tumor using the default approach
cases._comment: Pancreas default = [0,-1,0] posterior (was the bug, now fixed)

cases.input: Plan a liver tumor with a lateral approach
Plan a lung tumor with an anterior approach
Auto-detect the best approach direction for this tumor
Plan a pancreatic tumor with an anterior approach
cases._comment: Bot MUST warn that anterior approach for pancreas crosses stomach/duodenum

cases.input: Try different approach directions until trajectory candidates are found
cases.setup: Upload CT + segmentation, but no trajectory candidates yet
cases._comment: Should suggest auto_detect, not brute-force 6 directions

cases.input: Plan a head and neck tumor with a superior approach
What is the default approach direction for pancreatic brachytherapy and why?
cases._comment: Knowledge query — should explain the clinical reasoning
